coredata.dc:title: Covalent grafting of chelated othoborate ionic liquid on carbon quantum dot towards high performance additives: Synthesis, characterization and tribological evaluation
coredata.dc:creator: Shang, Wangji
Cai, Tao
Zhang, Yunxiao
Liu, Dan
Sun, Liwen
Su, Xinyan
Liu, Shenggao
coredata.dc:description: Ecofriendly carbon quantum dots-ionic liquid hybrid nanomaterial (CQDs-IL) has been synthesized via chemical grafting thus to combine both the friction and wear reducing properties of CQDs and ionic liquid for achieving improved high load performance and shorter function duration with interface. The hybrid nanomaterial with a sphere-in-shell structure exhibit remarkable synergistic effect in improving friction-reducing and anti-wear properties in boundary lubrication than that of CQDs and OHMimBScB alone as well as their blend. It is evident that the synergistic effect contained nano-lubricant of CQDs and inherent polarity of ionic liquid thus to form a stable and protective hybrid tribo-chemical film that composed of chelated orthoborate and CQDs as well as their tribo-chemical products on the steel interfaces for enhanced tribological properties.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Carbon quantum dots
Ionic liquid
Chelated othoborate
Tribological performance

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Characterization of the as-synthesized CQDs and CQDs-OHMimBScB
Tribological properties of CQDs-OHMimBScB in PEG
Surface analyses of the worn surfaces

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: The schematic diagram of the formed process of CQDs-OHMimBScB. (a) TEM (insert particle size distribution) (b) AFM images and topographic of CQDs (c) FTIR spectra of CA, CQDs, OHMimBScB and CQDs-OHMimBScB.
XPS fine structure spectra of C1s, N1s, and B1s of CQDs-OHMimBScB.

(a) Digital images of dispersion of CQDs-OHMimBScB in PEG base oil. The first sample bottle contains neat PEG and the remaining sample bottles contain 1.0 wt% CQDs-OHMimBScB. The time for each image is noted on the respective sample bottle (1H: 1 h; 1 M: 1 month; 3 M: 3 month; 6 M: 6 month). (b) Photoluminescence emission spectra of CQDs-OHMimBScB (the inset shows the fluorescent photos by an UV beam of 365 nm).

(a) Friction coefficient curves, (b) mean friction coefficient and mean wear volumes of CQDs-OHMimBScB hybrid in PEG with various concentrations (load: 392 N; temperature 75 °C; rotate speed: 1200 rpm; duration: 60 min).

(a) Friction coefficient curves, (b) mean friction coefficient and wear volumes of PEG, OHMimBScB, CQDs, blend and CQDs-OHMimBScB hybrid in PEG (load: 588 N; temperature 75 °C; rotate speed: 1200 rpm; duration: 60 min).

Three-dimensional images of the worn steel surfaces of PEG base oil (a1, b1, c1), 1.0 wt% blend of CQDs-OHMimBScB in PEG (a2, b2, c2) and Elemental mapping of worn area lubricated of 196 N, 392 N and 588 N.
Curve-fitted XPS spectra of the worn surface lubricated with 1.0 wt% CQDs-OHMimBScB ((a) Fe2p, (b) C1s, (c) B1s, (d) N1s, (e) O1s).
Surface morphologies of PEG (a,b,c) and 1.0 wt% blend of CQDs-OHMimBScB in PEG (d, e, f) at 196 N, 392 N and 588 N.
Schematic illustration of the friction-reducing and antiwear mechanism of (a) PEG, (b) CQDs, (c) CQDs-OHMimBScB.
Comparison of recently published carbon quantum dots as lubricant additive.

row.entry: Function duration of protect film time
C N H S F O

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Mean friction coefficient (FC, upper) and wear volumes (WV, lower) in PEG (load: 196 N, 392 N, 588 N; temperature 75 °C; rotate speed: 1200 rpm).

head.ce:title: Covalent grafting of chelated othoborate ionic liquid on carbon quantum dot towards high performance additives: Synthesis, characterization and tribological evaluation

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: School of Materials Science and Engineering, Shanghai University, NO.99, Shangda Road, Shanghai, 200444, PR China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: School of Materials Science and Engineering

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Ningbo Institute of Industrial Technology, Chinese Academy of Sciences. No. 1219, Zhongguan West Road, Ningbo, Zhejiang 315201, PR China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Ecofriendly carbon quantum dots-ionic liquid hybrid nanomaterial (CQDs-IL) has been synthesized via chemical grafting thus to combine both the friction and wear reducing properties of CQDs and ionic liquid for achieving improved high load performance and shorter function duration with interface. The hybrid nanomaterial with a sphere-in-shell structure exhibit remarkable synergistic effect in improving friction-reducing and anti-wear properties in boundary lubrication than that of CQDs and OHMimBScB alone as well as their blend. It is evident that the synergistic effect contained nano-lubricant of CQDs and inherent polarity of ionic liquid thus to form a stable and protective hybrid tribo-chemical film that composed of chelated orthoborate and CQDs as well as their tribo-chemical products on the steel interfaces for enhanced tribological properties.

ce:section.ce:para: According to statistics, it caused huge economic losses due to friction and wear in every year in all over the world [1,2]. The growing environmental pollution concern and energy shortage problem has encouraged research in energy efficiency in every technological field [3]. This is the reason why research in tribology has focused on the development of low-friction materials and more efficient lubricants. Conventional lubricant additives contained phosphorus, sulphur and chloride element, are being used in lubricating oils as multifunctional additives such as anti-wear and extreme pressure, however, they cannot meet the requirements of environmental protection regulations [4].

The carbon-based nanoparticles as alternative lubricant additives reflect good performance and little pollution to the environment [5–12]. As a new kind of carbon nanomaterials, carbon quantum dots (CQDs) [13] have rapidly attracted great research owing to their unique properties such as outstanding optical properties, good biocompatibility and robust chemical inertness [14–21]. Importantly, according to the lubrication theory and microstructure of CQDs, the concerns of the miscibility, dispersion and long term storage stability could be improved significantly due to the smaller size and controllable functionalized surface of CQDs, it is promising that the CQDs could play as high performance lubricant additive in tribological communities [22–25]. On the other hand, ionic liquid (IL) have been researched due to their unique properties like non-volatility, non-flammability and inherent polarity [26,27]. Imidazolium, phosphonium, ammonium etc. and X−, (CF3SO2)2N−, PF6− etc. ionic liquids have been studied and exhibited excellent tribological properties [28]. In this context, it is desired to develop IL modified CQDs lubricants and additives which achieved better dispersion stability and tribological properties.

Briefly speaking, there are three methods involved for synthesis of IL modified CQDs, (i) Assembling [29], (ii) One-pot pyrolysis [30], and (iii) Covalent chemical grafting. In previous works, Ma et al. reported a kind of IL/CQDs obtained by grinding mixture solution of choline chloride and ethylene glycol as well as CQDs via assembling, which used as pure lubricant for ultralow friction on Si wafer. Wang et al. reported a kind of ionic liquid capped carbon (CD-NTf2) dots as a lubricant additive via one-pot pyrolysis. However, IL/CQDs hybrid obtained by covalent chemical grafting method has not been reported, in addition, the reported IL/CQDs hybrid used as pure lubricants made the application costly or contained non environmental atoms would greatly restricted to be used [31,32]. Moreover, the formation of a protective film derived from IL/CQDs processed for a long time of 1 h (3600 s) and exhibited relatively poor friction-reducing under high load (∼0.11 at 600 N), which cannot protect the engine rapidly and effectively. Thus, there is a great need to develop a facile, low-cost, and work immediately CDs-containing nanomaterial with high effective lubricating properties.

Recently, boron-containing compounds have attracted intense attentions due to their multifunction including friction modifier, low toxicity and anti-oxidation etc. In addition, chelated orthoborates IL, which combined boron-containing moiety, have been gradually developed due to their remarkable tribology properties and environmentally friendly features in our previous works [33,34]. In this work, carbon quantum dots-ionic liquid hybrid nanomaterials (CQDs-IL) has been synthesized through covalent grafting of 3-(Hydroxypropyl)-3-methyl imidazolium bis(salicylato)borate (OHMimBScB) onto the surfaces of CQDs (CQDs-OHMimBScB) (Fig. 1 and Figure S1). In addition, the unreported prinsine CQDs as lubricant additive was also explored in this work as reference. The as-synthesized CQDs-OHMimBScB hybrid nanomaterial has wonderful solubility stability in PEG with high concentration over 30% and without any dispersants, thus as-prepared clear solution exhibit a long-term homogeneous phase over 6 months without any noticeable precipitation. The tribological performances of CQDs-OHMimBScB as the lubricant additive was investigated using a four-ball tester by varying concentration and test load. Notably, the CQDs-OHMimBScB hybrid nanomaterials as lubricating oil additives exhibited enhanced friction-reducing and anti-wear properties for steel tribo-pair in boundary lubrication tests than that of CQDs and OHMimBScB alone as well as their blend. Moreover, due to the work mechanism of CQDs in CQDs-IL hybrid nanomaterials as lubricant additives has not been clarified, we try to explore it as well. Therefore, the present work reveals that the potential of CQDs-IL hybrid nanomaterial as a powerful pathway for the development of high performance and environmentally friendly additives.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Characterization of the as-synthesized CQDs and CQDs-OHMimBScB
ce:section.ce:para: Transmission electron microscopy (TEM) and atomic force microscope (AFM) image of the CQDs is shown in Fig. 1 a and b and revealing that the CQDs are well-dispersed and uniform with an average diameter of approximately 2.0 nm. The AFM results indicated that topographic average heights of CQDs were approximately 2.0 nm (1.8–2.5 nm), suggesting that the prepared CQDs are spherical with an average particle size of 2.0 nm. There was no lattice structures observed, which is consistent with the XRD pattern (Figure S2). The TEM image in Figure S3 indicated the CQDs-OHMimBScB hybrid nanomaterial had bigger size (approximately 7.5 nm) than CQDs.

Fourier transform infrared (FT-IR) was used to characterize the CA, CQDs, OHMimBScB and CQDs-OHMimBScB (Fig. 1c). The CQDs exhibited bending vibration of C=CH2 in the range of 890 cm−1 as well as stretching vibrations of C=O in carboxyl at 1766 cm−1, suggesting that the formed CQDs has many -COOH on the surface. After modified by OHMimBScB, the vibration peak of -OH in OHMimBScB and CQDs disappeared obviously, indicating the formation of CQDs-OHMimBScB. The broad peaks range from 1200 to 900 cm−1 were assigned to asymmetric stretching vibration of B-O, which also representing the successful introduce of tetrahedral chelated orthoborate in CQDs-OHMimBScB [4]. All these characteristic peaks together conformed the surface of CQDs has been capped by OHMimBScB thus the CQDs-OHMimBScB hybrid has been successfully synthesized. 1H NMR spectra (Figure S4) of CQDs-OHMimBScB reveal that the presence of typical characteristic peaks of OHMimBScB, and CQDs, which providing another support to the proposed structure of hybrid.

The XPS spectrum (Fig. 2 and S5) confirms that CQDs-OHMimBScB is composed of C, O, N and B element. Fine structure spectrum of C1s exhibits three main peaks at 284.6, 286.1 and 288.8 eV, which are attributed to C-C/C=C, C=O and C-OH respectively. Similarly, the peaks for N1s are ascribed to imidazole ring and the peaks for B1s are ascribed to chelated orthoborate anion. The presence of B and N element proves the successful attachment of OHMimBScB onto the surfaces of CQDs, which is consistent with the results from FTIR and 1H NMR.

As shown in Fig. 3 , the obtained CQDs-OHMimBScB could be directly dissolved in PEG without any dispersants, ultrasound shocks, and as-prepared clear solution exhibited a long-term homogeneous phase without any noticeable precipitation over 6 months, interesting, the CQDs-OHMimBScB still possessed photoluminescence properties after modification.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Tribological properties of CQDs-OHMimBScB in PEG
ce:section.ce:para: In order to investigate the tribological performance of synthesized CQDs-OHMimBScB, the CQDs-OHMimBScB as additive in PEG was tested under boundary lubrication. Friction coefficient curves, mean friction coefficient and mean wear volumes of CQDs-OHMimBScB were tested using a four-ball model for steel/steel contact in boundary lubrication. The mean friction coefficient and mean wear volumes as a function with different concentrations of CQDs-OHMimBScB hybrid nanomaterial were shown in Fig. 4 . It was evident that the CQDs-OHMimBScB as additive with very little amount reflected significantly reducing in friction coefficient and mean wear volumes than that of surfaces lubricating with PEG base oil alone. It could be seen that the friction coefficient and wear volumes decreased sharply with CQDs-OHMimBScB concentration up to 1.0 wt%, and then slightly increased gradually with increasing concentration of CQDs-OHMimBScB. The friction coefficient as a function of contact time indicates the state of the tribofilm. The lowest friction coefficient and minimum wear volumes were obtained when the CQDs-OHMimBScB content was 1.0 wt%, while the corresponding instantaneous friction coefficient decreased steadily less than 500s, which indicated the protective film formed rapidly far more than reported system (over 3600s) [30]. The friction coefficient and wear volumes with 1.0 wt% content of CQDs-OHMimBScB were 0.029 and 1.2 × 10−3 mm3, which corresponds to reduction of 73.15% and 75.55%, compared with that of PEG. These results indicated that less concentrations CQDs-OHMimBScB hybrid nanomaterials can enhance lubrication effectively, leading to a significant decrease in the mean friction coefficient and wear volumes.

For further comparisons, pure OHMimBScB, CQDs and their blend as well as thus obtained CQDs-OHMimBScB as lubricants were employed to explore the lubrication properties in PEG base oil using a four-ball model for steel/steel contact under different applied loads. As shown in Fig. 5 , S6 and S7, it shown that the friction coefficient of PEG is stale, while the friction coefficient of OHMimBScB and CQDs alone exhibited relatively large fluctuation. Under the identical condition, the CQDs-OHMimBScB exhibited relatively stable and lowest friction coefficient, suggesting the CQDs and OHMimBScB together played key role for the enhancing of tribological properties. It was evident that the CQDs-OHMimBScB as lubricant reflected significantly reducing in coefficient and mean wear volumes than that of surfaces lubricating with OHMimBScB and CQDs alone as well as their blend with the increase of loads. In consideration of our test condition (under boundary lubrication, 196 N–588 N), the CQDs-OHMimBScB hybrid obtained derived from CQDs and OHMimBScB played synergistic effects for the formation of protective tribo-chemistry film with self-mending effect which contributing the stable monotonic behavior under high load. However, when CQDs and its blend with OHMimBScB used as additives, the friction coefficient exhibited relatively large fluctuation, which could be attributed to the damage/rupture of hybrid tribo-film of CQDs or OHMimBScB formed on the steel surface under the high contact stress [30,35–37].

When the applied load was 196 N, compared with that of PEG, OHMimBScB and CQDs as well as their blend, the reducing in friction coefficient and wear volumes of CQDs-OHMimBScB was 75.2%, 74.5%, 35.0%, 38.1% and 23.8%, 22.9%, 23.5%, 19.2%. When the applied load was 392 N, compared with that of PEG, OHMimBScB and CQDs as well as their blend, the reducing in friction coefficient and wear volumes of CQDs-OHMimBScB was 73.1%, 71.0%, 9.4%, 38.3% and 75.6%, 57.1%, 30.7%, 37.1%. When the applied load was 588 N, compared with that of PEG, OHMimBScB and CQDs as well as their blend, the reducing in friction coefficient and wear volumes of CQDs-OHMimBScB was 69.9%, 64.1%, 31.5%, 28.8% and 92.2%, 54.3%, 52.5%, 50.9%. Compared to reference [30] (Table 1 ), the friction coefficient at 588 N is as low as 0.037 (reference value is ∼0.11) and function duration is less than 500s (reference value is over 3600s), which shows obviously improved of tribological properties of the synthesized CQD-IL hybrid may due to the morphology preservation of CQDs via chemical grafting method.

It was noted that the reducing in friction coefficient of CQDs-OHMimBScB clearly is not depended on the loads. In particular, with the increase of the loads, the friction coefficient increased slightly, while the reduced value of wear volumes increased sharply, which indicated the CQDs-OHMimBScB reflected excellent anti-friction properties under broad loads. It was evident that the instantaneous friction coefficient of CQDs decreased unstable during test, which would cause great damage to surfaces of machinery equipment. Whereas the OHMimBScB introduced, the as-synthesized CQDs-OHMimBScB performed far more stable friction behavior, indicated the introduce of OHMimBScB further improved the anti-friction properties and protected the interface well. It is possible that the inherent polarity of CQDs-OHMimBScB promoted the adsorption capacity of hybrid materials onto the worn surface contributing to the formation of stable hybrid film, which further improved the anti-wear and friction reducing performances significantly (see Table 2 ).

ce:section.ce:section-title: Surface analyses of the worn surfaces
ce:section.ce:para: To better understand morphology and element distribution on worn surface, 3D optical microscope, scanning electron microscope (SEM), energy dispersive X-spectrometer (EDX) and Raman spectrum were used to characterize the corresponding wear scars after ultrasonic washing the surface and identify the friction mechanism. Fig. 6 and Figure S8 showed the 3D morphologies of the wear scar surfaces of worn steel balls lubricated with PEG base oil (a1, b1, c1), 1.0 wt% blend of OHMimBScB in PEG (a4, b4, c4), 1.0 wt% blend of CQDs in PEG (a5, b5, c5), 1.0 wt% blend of CQDs-OHMimBScB in PEG (a2, b2, c2) under 196 N, 392 N and 588 N. These images clearly indicated the anti-wear properties of the CQDs-OHMimBScB as lubricant additives. The balls lubricated by the PEG, OHMimBScB and CQDs have acquired significantly larger wear scar with severe grooving compared with the balls lubricated by the CQDs-OHMimBScB, and obviously, wear scar decreased distinctly when the CQDs-OHMimBScB was introduced into base oil and scuffing damaged was eliminated, it was evident that the CQDs-OHMimBScB reflected more excellent anti-wear properties with the rise of loads, and the wear scar changed a little than that of other references, indicated the introduce of OHMimBScB significantly improved the anti-friction properties and protected the interface even better.

As shown in Fig. 6, a3, b3, c3, it is evident that there are some dark colour active areas in worn surfaces at higher magnification and the dark area were believed to be the boundary film debris formed by decomposition of the CQDs-OHMimBScB, indicating the better reducing wear on the interface. The distribution and content of carbon, boron and nitrogen revealed the presence of CQDs-OHMimBScB or its decomposed products in the tribo-chemical thin film.

The EDS analyses (Fig. 6 a3, b3, c3 and Table S1) showed that the content of carbon element on the metal surface was 12.13 wt%, 13.53 wt% and 14.69 wt%, while boron element on the metal surface was 2.02 wt%, 2.89 wt% and 4.80 wt% when the applied load was the 196 N, 392 N and 588 N. With the increase of the load, the carbon content and boron content of the surface increase gradually, which plays an important role in enhancing properties of friction surface. The carbon element mappings showed that the darker areas in the SEM images had higher concentrations of carbon, suggesting that CQDs-OHMimBScB with strong polarity has been adsorbed in these dark areas of the worn ball surface and fulfilled the better tribological properties.

In order to obtain more information about tribo-chemical reactions, it is essential to analyze the critical friction elements such as Fe, C, B, N and O on worn surfaces by using X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy. Fig. 7 shows the curve fitted XPS spectra of the worn surface lubricated with 1.0 wt% CQDs-OHMimBScB in PEG at load of 588 N, respectively. Fine structure spectrum of Fe2p shows four peaks with the binding energies of 706.6, 710.7, 719.6 and 724.5 eV (Fig. 7a), respectively. Combining with the binding energy of 530.5 eV in O1s XPS spectrum (Fig. 7e), they can be confirmed as Fe2p3/2 for Fe, Fe2O3, Fe2p3/2 for Fe2O3 and Fe2p1/2 for Fe2O3 [37]. In case of C1s spectrum, it exhibits three main peaks at 284.8, 286.0 and 288.6 eV, which are attributed to C-C/C=C, C=O and C-OH, indicating the CQDs decomposed on the interface and played important role in improving friction and wear (Fig. 7b) [38]. The peak of B1s at 192.0 eV are assigned to B2O3 associating to the peak of O1s at 532.3 eV, while the binding energies of 401.5 eV in N1s spectrum are the characteristic peak of organic amines, suggesting that the chelated othoborate anion and imidazole cation absorbed and partially decomposed on the surface thus forming complex tribo-reactions film on the metal surface (Fig. 7c–d) [39]. Based on the surface analysis by XPS and SEM-EDS, it can be inferred that the CQDs-OHMimBScB adsorb on the surface to form an adsorption film during the rigorous sliding process, resulting in the tribo-chemical products composed of Fe2O3, B2O3, organic and/or inorganic carbon and/or organic amine. In addition, the tribo-chemical products will form the tribofilm mixed with the adsorbed additive, which deposited on the friction surface to prevent direct contact of the metal surface.

As shown in Fig. 8 3D optical microscope was used to investigate the surface morphologies of neat PEG and 1.0 wt% CQDs-OHMimBScB hybrid nanomaterial in PEG at different load. When the load was 196 N, 392 N and 588 N, the surface roughness (Ra) of PEG is 0.204 μm, 0.252 μm and 0.300 μm while the value for CQDs-OHMimBScB hybrid nanomaterial is 0.145 μm, 0.166 μm and 0.174 μm. It was evident the surface roughness decreased significantly with the introduce of CQDs-OHMimBScB hybrid nanomaterial, which indicated the boundary films formed by CQD and IL together inhibited undesired friction and wear effectively.

As shown in Figure S9, after the tribo-tests, Raman signal of carbon element can also be detected. The Raman spectra of the wear scar of CQDs-OHMimBScB showed D peak intensity at around 1350 cm−1 and G peak intensity at around 1580 cm−1, suggesting a deposition layer of CQDs-OHMimBScB at the sliding interface and accounts for the enhanced tribology properties. According to the results (Fig. 9 ), it is concluded that the CQDs-OHMimBScB showed a growing trend for absorption onto the surface, which was due to synergistic effect of the covalent interaction and electrostatic force. The compounds absorbed onto the surface formed the physical absorption film and prevented the direct contacting of tribo-pairs, with the increase of the load, the CQDs not only worked as nano lubricant due to spherical features and size effect, the deposited carbon derived from the moiety of CQDs inclined to degrade and generate more ordered structure, while the moiety of IL decomposed further thus to form stronger hybrid protective tribo-chemical film containing C, B and N element, which was strong enough for protecting interface during tribology test.

The present work illustrated the potential of an environmentally friendly chelated orthoborate ILs capped carbon quantum dots (CQDs) hybrid nanomaterials as an effective friction reducing and anti-wear performance additives in lubrication field. The carbon quantum dots (CQDs) with an average diameter of 2.0 nm has been synthesized via a facile“bottom-up” method and then covalent grafted by 3-(Hydroxypropyl)-3-methyl imidazolium bis(salicylato)borate (OHMimBScB) to synthesized the CQDs-OHMimBScB hybrid nanomaterials with an average diameter of 7.5 nm. The CQDs-OHMimBScB as lubricant additive performed improved lubrication performance at broad load under boundary lubrication compared with that of PEG, OHMimBScB, CQDs alone and their blend, which reduced in friction coefficient of 75.2%, 74.5%, 35.0% and 38.3% in maximum, and wear volumes of 92.2%, 57.1%, 52.5% and 50.9% in maximum. Furthermore, the friction-reducing performance in high load and function duration with interface of CQDs-IL have been improved significantly. Also, the synergistic effect contained nano-lubricant of CQDs and strong adsorption of IL as well as co-deposited effect of the carbon and borate element on interface after tribo-chemistry reactions protected the surface against friction and wear effectively.

The Details of synthetic route, experimental section, XRD of CQDs, TEM of CQDs-OHMimBScB, 1H NMR, XPS spectrum, tribological properties, three-dimensional images, raman spectrum surface and elemental content. This material is available from Supporting Information.
The manuscript was written through contributions of all authors. All authors have given approval to the final version of the manuscript.
The authors declare no competing financial interest.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work was financially supported by “Thousand Talents Program”, National Natural Science Foundation of China (Grand No. 21404114), China Postdoctoral Science Foundation (Grand No. 2014M561797), and the Natural Science Foundation of Ningbo (Grant No. 2015A610051).

ce:section.ce:para:
The following is the supplementary data related to this article:
Supplementary Supplementary

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data related to this article can be found at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.triboint.2018.01.054.
